title: austrolycopodium erectum
intro: Austrolycopodium erectum, also known as the New Caledonian spike rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Restionaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Austrolycopodium erectum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the dried plant material is steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
